zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord William Phillips Earl於1559年4月2日出生在英國史洛普爾盧德洛福時，他的父親Sir Thomas Phillips II MP58歲，母親Johane Smith37歲。他於1584年11月2日與Margaret Bartlett在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1659年4月2日在英國武斯特斯爾伍斯特享年100歲。

nameMeanings.name: Phillips
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Dutch, North German, and Jewish (western Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name   Philip  . In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages (see   Philips  ).

variants.name: Phillipps
meanings: English, Scottish and Dutch: variant of   Phillips  .

variants.name: Philipps
meanings: English, Scottish, North German, and Dutch: variant of   Phillips  .

variants.name: Philip
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Varughese, Mathai, Babu, Reji, Anil, Binu, Neena, Prema, Raju, Reena, Susheela, Aleykutty.

Scottish, English, German, Dutch, and Jewish: from the Greek personal name Philippos (from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’). In the New Testament this name is borne by one of the apostles; it was also borne by various other early Christian saints. It owes part of its popularity to the medieval romances about Alexander the Great, whose father was Philip of Macedon. As a Jewish name, it represents a borrowing of the personal name from Christians. As a Highland Scottish surname, it represents an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Spanish Felipe , Catalan Felip, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or Croatian Filip , Albanian Filipi , and their derivatives (see examples at Philips ). The name Philip is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Philipose and Pothen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Phillip .
French (southern) and Breton (Finistère): variant of   Philippe  .

variants.name: Phelps
meanings: Scottish and English (southwestern): variant of   Phillips  .

History: The brothers George and William Phelps emigrated from Gloucestershire, England, to Dorchester, MA, c. 1630. Five years later they moved to Windsor, CT. George's sixth-generation descendant, Anson Greene Phelps (1781–1853), rose from being a penniless orphan to the status of a major industrialist and a prominent CT philanthropist.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord William Phillips Earl was born on 2 April 1559, in Ludlow, Shropshire, England, his father, Sir Thomas Phillips II MP, was 58 and his mother, Johane Smith, was 37. He married Margaret Bartlett on 2 November 1584, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 2 April 1659, in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 100.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord William Phillips Earlが1559年4月2日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアルドロウで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Phillips II MPは58歳，母Johane Smithは37歳であった。1584年11月2日にイギリスロンドンでMargaret Bartlettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1659年4月2日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターで100歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord William Phillips Earl(이)가 1559년 4월 2일 영국 슈럽실르 루들로에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Phillips II MP(은)는 58세였고, 그의 어머니 Johane Smith(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1584년 11월 2일 영국 런던에서 Margaret Bartlett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1659년 4월 2일 영국 워스스터실르 워스스터에서 100세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.2 April 1559–2 April 1659: 2 de abril de 1559–2 de abril de 1659
pt.Cleobury Mortimer, Shropshire, England,: Cleobury Mortimer, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
Cleobury Mortimer, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Saint Augustine Watling Street, London,: St Augustine with St Faith Watling Street, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord William Phillips Earl nasceu em 2 de abril de 1559, em Ludlow, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Phillips II MP, tinha 58 anos e sua mãe, Johane Smith, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Bartlett em 2 de novembro de 1584, em London, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 2 de abril de 1659, em Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 100 anos.

ru.Saint Augustine Watling Street, London,: ст аугустаин вит ст феит ватлинг стрит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord William Phillips Earl родился 2 апреля 1559 г. в местности лудлоу, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Phillips II MP было 58 лет, а матери, которую звали Johane Smith, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Bartlett 2 ноября 1584 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 2 апреля 1659 г. в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 100 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord William Phillips Earl nació el 2 de abril de 1559, en Ludlow, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas Phillips II MP, tenía 58 años y su mamá, Johane Smith, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Margaret Bartlett el 2 de noviembre de 1584, en London, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 2 de abril de 1659, en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 100 años.
